methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $28M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Orbit Fab is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Daniel Faber and Jeremy Schiel that has raised $28.5M to date. The valuation exercise relies on a peer universe of 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-stage peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs, though a sufficiently narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier does not exist to support median-based multiples.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable triangulation method here. Using total capital raised as a proxy baseline of $28M and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $57M. This method reflects observed progression patterns within the venture capital continuum for aerospace companies. Public-comp anchoring using the Space & Aerospace median P/S of 25.8x across the 6-ticker set cannot be applied absent revenue disclosure from the target. The private peer set, while qualitatively relevant, lacks sufficient homogeneous datapoints for statistically meaningful median extraction.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $48M to $66M, with $57M as the median point. This single-method triangulation carries material uncertainty given the narrow empirical foundation. Key sensitivities include target revenue (which would unlock public-comp anchoring) and the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier to Orbit Fab's specific capital efficiency and market timing relative to peer progression patterns.
